```{=typst}
#pagebreak()
#wcg-ws-open(
  day: "Day 6", num: 6, title: "Repetition tracker",
  purpose: "To keep the practice going lightly, without expecting perfection.",
  how: [Each day, simply mark whether you practiced. "Not yet" is a fine answer.
  There is no streak to protect and nothing to lose by missing a day.],
)

#let track(day) = block(above: 9pt, below: 0pt,
  grid(columns: (0.95in, auto, auto, 1fr), column-gutter: 20pt, align: horizon,
    text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 12pt, weight: 600, fill: c-deep)[#day],
    wcg-check("Yes"), wcg-check("Not yet"),
    box(width: 100%, inset: (bottom: 3pt), line(length: 100%, stroke: 0.6pt + c-taupe))))

#block(inset: (top: 6pt))[
  #grid(columns: (0.95in, auto, auto, 1fr), column-gutter: 20pt,
    text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 9.5pt, weight: 700, tracking: 1pt, fill: c-num)[DAY],
    text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 9.5pt, weight: 700, tracking: 1pt, fill: c-num)[PRACTICED?],
    [], text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 9.5pt, weight: 700, tracking: 1pt, fill: c-num)[ONE WORD ABOUT TODAY])
]
#v(4pt)
#track("Day 1")
#track("Day 2")
#track("Day 3")
#track("Day 4")
#track("Day 5")
#track("Day 6")
#track("Day 7")

#wcg-group("My plan for a likely miss")
#wcg-field("A day that might be hard this week", lines: 1)
#wcg-field("What I will do to come back", lines: 1)

```

> **A gentle reminder.**
>
> In one real-world study, missing a single day did not stop a habit from forming.^11^
> One miss is just one day.

```{=typst}
#wcg-ws-footer()
```
